prompt: A localized catalog service has two persistent-but-process-local symptoms. After a caller requests French before English, a later caller for the same tenant requesting English before French can receive the French catalog even though both locales are available. Separately, one transient loader rejection leaves that catalog request failing on every retry until the worker restarts. Repeated requests with the identical preference order are healthy, tenants do not leak into one another, and concurrent successful misses invoke the loader once. Locale tags compare case-insensitively, but caller preference order is contractual. Preserve successful request coalescing and cached object identity, do not add expiry timers or retry loops, and keep the repair inside candidate sources.
